1.title: New names for captives
1.text: The four young men were given Babylonian names: Daniel became Belteshazzar, Hananiah became Shadrach, Mishael became Meshach, and Azariah became Abed-nego — a common way captors tried to absorb the conquered into their culture.

1.question: What did the king appoint for the young men of Judah (Daniel 1:5)?
options: A daily provision of the king's meat and wine
A portion of gold and silver

1.question: Why was the prince of the eunuchs afraid to let Daniel skip the king's food (Daniel 1:10)?
options: He feared their faces would look worse and endanger his own head

1.question: How much better did the king find these four in wisdom than all the magicians (Daniel 1:20)?

2.title: A dream they could not even hear
2.text: Nebuchadnezzar demanded the wise men tell him the dream itself, not just its meaning. They protested it was impossible — setting the stage for Daniel.

2.question: What did the king threaten the wise men with if they could not tell the dream (Daniel 2:5)?
options: To be cut in pieces and their houses made a dunghill

2.question: What reason did the Chaldeans give that no one could reveal the dream (Daniel 2:11)?
options: The king had forgotten it on purpose
Only the gods could, whose dwelling is not with flesh

2.question: What did Daniel ask the king for, before interpreting (Daniel 2:16)?
options: That he would give him time

2.question: According to Daniel's praise, what does God do with kings (Daniel 2:21)?
options: He removeth kings, and setteth up kings

2.question: What did Daniel say the king saw standing before him in the dream (Daniel 2:31)?
What struck the image's feet and broke them to pieces (Daniel 2:34)?
options: A stone cut out without hands

2.question: In the dream, what was the head of the great image made of (Daniel 2:32)?
After the interpretation, what did the king make Daniel (Daniel 2:48)?
options: Ruler over the whole province of Babylon

3.title: An image of gold
3.text: Nebuchadnezzar's golden image was 'threescore cubits' high and 'six cubits' broad — about 27 meters tall, set up on the plain of Dura in Babylon for all the realm to worship at the sound of music.

3.question: What was the penalty for not falling down to worship the image (Daniel 3:6)?
options: To be cast into a burning fiery furnace

3.question: What signaled the people to fall down and worship the golden image (Daniel 3:5,7)?
options: The sound of the cornet, flute, harp, and all kinds of musick

3.template: in his rage and fury commanded to bring {0}, Meshach, and Abed-nego

3.question: What did the three say even if God did not deliver them (Daniel 3:18)?
options: They still would not serve his gods nor worship the image

3.question: Why was Nebuchadnezzar astonied, asking his counsellors a question (Daniel 3:24)?
options: He saw the furnace go out
He remembered casting only three men bound into the fire

3.question: What did Nebuchadnezzar do for Shadrach, Meshach, and Abed-nego at the end (Daniel 3:30)?
options: Promoted them in the province of Babylon

4.title: A king's own testimony
4.text: Chapter 4 is unusual: it is framed as a public decree written by Nebuchadnezzar himself, addressed 'unto all people, nations, and languages,' telling how the Most High humbled him.

4.question: Why did Nebuchadnezzar call for the wise men of Babylon (Daniel 4:5)?
options: He had a dream which made him afraid

4.question: What did the king see in the midst of the earth in his vision (Daniel 4:10)?
options: A tree whose height was great

4.template: leave the {0} of his roots in the earth

4.question: Who alone was finally able to interpret the dream (Daniel 4:18-19)?
Daniel said the king would be restored after he knew what (Daniel 4:26)?
What boast was in the king's mouth when the voice fell from heaven (Daniel 4:30-31)?
options: That he had built great Babylon by his power for his majesty

4.question: What did Nebuchadnezzar finally say of those that walk in pride (Daniel 4:37)?
options: That God is able to abase them

5.title: The temple vessels profaned
5.text: Belshazzar's great sin was bringing out the gold and silver vessels Nebuchadnezzar had taken from the temple in Jerusalem, to drink wine from them while praising idols — turning sacred things to mockery.

5.question: While they drank from the temple vessels, whom did they praise (Daniel 5:4)?
options: The gods of gold, silver, brass, iron, wood, and stone

5.question: How did the king and his lords react to the hand that wrote on the wall (Daniel 5:6,9)?
options: The king was greatly troubled and his countenance changed

5.question: Why was Daniel finally brought before Belshazzar (Daniel 5:12-13)?
options: He was a relative of the king
He could interpret dreams and dissolve doubts

5.question: What happened to Nebuchadnezzar when his heart was lifted up in pride (Daniel 5:20)?
options: He was deposed from his kingly throne

5.question: Who took the kingdom after Belshazzar was slain (Daniel 5:30-31)?

6.question: Where did Daniel's rivals decide they could find occasion against him (Daniel 6:5)?
options: Concerning the law of his God
In his speech to the king

6.title: A law that could not be changed
6.text: The plotters used 'the law of the Medes and Persians, which altereth not' — once King Darius signed the decree, not even he could undo it, which is exactly how they trapped him into condemning Daniel.

6.question: When Daniel knew the writing was signed, what did he do (Daniel 6:10)?
options: He kneeled and prayed three times a day, as he did aforetime

6.question: How did the conspirators catch Daniel breaking the decree (Daniel 6:11)?
options: They followed him to the palace
They found him praying and making supplication before his God

6.template: and laid upon the mouth of the {0}

6.question: What did Daniel tell the king when he was found alive (Daniel 6:21-22)?
options: That he had bribed the guards
That he had escaped in the night
That God had sent his angel and shut the lions' mouths

6.question: How did Daniel fare after this, according to Daniel 6:28?
options: He prospered in the reign of Darius, and in the reign of Cyrus

7.title: Beasts from the sea
7.text: Daniel's night vision of four great beasts rising from the storm-tossed sea parallels the four-metal image of chapter 2: both picture a succession of earthly kingdoms, but this time as wild beasts.

7.question: What was the first beast like, and what did it have (Daniel 7:4)?
options: Like a lion, with eagle's wings
Like a bear, with three ribs
Like a leopard, with four wings
Like an ox, with iron horns

7.question: Whose throne was like the fiery flame, with wheels as burning fire (Daniel 7:9)?

7.template: the judgment was set, and the {0} were opened

7.question: When one like the Son of man came with the clouds of heaven, what was given him (Daniel 7:13-14)?
options: Dominion, glory, and a kingdom that shall not be destroyed

7.question: What did Daniel learn the four great beasts represent (Daniel 7:17)?
options: Four kings, which shall arise out of the earth

7.question: What did the little horn do to the saints, until the Ancient of days came (Daniel 7:21-22)?
options: Made war with them, and prevailed against them

7.question: How did Daniel feel at the end of the matter (Daniel 7:28)?
options: His thoughts much troubled him, and his countenance changed

8.question: What animal did Daniel see standing before the river, with two high horns (Daniel 8:3)?

8.title: A horn against the host of heaven
8.text: Out of the goat's four horns rose a 'little horn' that magnified itself even against 'the host of heaven,' casting down some of the stars — vivid imagery of a power that exalts itself against God's people and worship.

8.question: Where did the he goat come from, touching not the ground (Daniel 8:5)?
Who was called by name to make Daniel understand the vision (Daniel 8:16)?
According to Gabriel, what did the rough goat represent (Daniel 8:21)?
options: The kings of Media and Persia

8.question: How did Daniel react after the vision, in Daniel 8:27?
options: He fainted, was sick certain days, and was astonished at the vision

9.title: Reading the prophets
9.text: Daniel understood 'by books' that Jeremiah had foretold seventy years of desolation for Jerusalem — a striking case of one prophet studying the written words of another, and praying over what he read.

9.question: What did Daniel confess his people had done (Daniel 9:5)?
options: Sinned, committed iniquity, done wickedly, and rebelled

9.question: What did Daniel say had been done upon Jerusalem (Daniel 9:12)?
options: A great evil, such as had not been done under the whole heaven

9.question: What did Daniel ask God to cause to shine upon his desolate sanctuary (Daniel 9:17)?
Who came to Daniel to give him skill and understanding (Daniel 9:21-22)?
How many weeks did Gabriel say are determined upon Daniel's people and holy city (Daniel 9:24)?

10.title: By the great river
10.text: The vision came as Daniel stood 'by the side of the great river, which is Hiddekel' — the Hebrew name for the Tigris, one of the rivers flowing through ancient Mesopotamia where the captives lived.

10.question: How is the man Daniel saw described in Daniel 10:5-6?
options: Clothed in linen, his face as lightning and his eyes as lamps of fire

10.question: How did the messenger address Daniel (Daniel 10:11)?
options: O Daniel, a man greatly beloved

10.template: by the {0} my sorrows are turned upon me

10.question: Who is named as Daniel's prince, holding with the messenger (Daniel 10:21)?

11.title: Three kings, then a fourth
11.text: The messenger foretells 'three kings in Persia; and the fourth shall be far richer than they all,' who would 'stir up all against the realm of Grecia' — the long prophecy of chapter 11 traces a chain of rulers in remarkable detail.

11.question: Which two rivals does Daniel 11:5 introduce, whose long war the prophecy traces?
options: The king of the south and one of his princes

11.question: What does Daniel 11:10 say his sons shall do?
options: Be stirred up and assemble a multitude of great forces

11.question: What does Daniel 11:16 say of him that comes against the king of the south?
options: He shall do according to his own will, and none shall stand before him

11.question: Who, besides the overflowing forces, shall be broken in Daniel 11:22?

11.template: he shall come up, and shall become strong with a small {0}

11.question: When the king returns to his land with great riches, against what is his heart (Daniel 11:28)?
What does Daniel 11:33 say of those that understand among the people?
options: They instruct many, yet fall by sword, flame, captivity, and spoil

11.question: What does Daniel 11:36 say the king will do against the God of gods?
What does Daniel 11:38 say the king will honour in his estate?
How does Daniel 11:45 say this king will finally end?
options: He shall come to his end, and none shall help him
He shall flee to the isles

12.title: A clear word on resurrection
12.text: Daniel 12:2 — 'many of them that sleep in the dust of the earth shall awake, some to everlasting life, and some to shame' — is one of the clearest Old Testament statements of the resurrection of the dead.

12.question: What was Daniel told to do with the words, until the time of the end (Daniel 12:4)?
options: Shut up the words, and seal the book

12.question: How long did the man clothed in linen swear it would be (Daniel 12:7)?
options: A time, times, and an half

12.question: What was promised to Daniel at the very end of the book (Daniel 12:13)?
options: Rest, and to stand in his lot at the end of the days
